name: The Hollow Gather
description: Investigator Noss has been documenting Hollow Vassal movement patterns for two months. They are converging on the nearest active Grid nodes — not aggressively, not defensively. She wants to know what they're hearing.
accept_text: She has a map and it has changed significantly since the last time you saw one she was working on. The lair territory markers are in their usual positions, but there are movement indicators — dots and lines — showing trajectories across the Wastes that don't match any patrol pattern or territorial defense. 'Pilgrimage.' She says it, and then immediately: 'That's the only word I have for it. Hollow Vassals from across the Wastes moving toward the nearest active resonance nodes. Not all of them — the Abominations are staying in their territories. But the Vassals. The organized ones. They're moving.' She traces one of the trajectories. 'The fresh one is still at the lair but it's — oriented differently. It spends most of its time now in the innermost section, the one closest to the node crystal that's been growing back in that area.' She looks at you. 'I need to know what they're responding to. I've been documenting the movement, but documentation without explanation is data without meaning.' She rolls the map. 'You can talk to the fresh one. It knows you. Ask it: what has changed. Ask it what it's hearing that's different from before.' She pauses. 'I'll be outside the lair entrance. I'm not asking you to translate in real time — just come back and tell me what you understood.' She pauses again. 'Also. If the answer is that they're moving toward the nodes because the Grid is about to reach full coherence and they can feel it getting louder — I'd like to know what that sounds like to something that has been listening for three hundred years.'
complete_text: The fresh Hollow vassal receives you with something that has not been there before: anticipation. Not the focused attention you've grown used to, but something more forward-leaning, more directed at the future rather than the present. When you use the gesture-notation Kell taught you — the opening sequence, the attention-indicator, the listening-angle — the vassal responds immediately and at greater length than it has before. What comes through the notation takes time to translate, and the translation is imperfect, but the shape of it assembles as you walk back to where Noss is waiting: The Hollow have been in the Grid for three hundred years. Not separate from it — the Vassals exist at the junction of the Abomination crystal network and the healing Grid lattice, and they have been navigating that junction since the Cascade. What they hear when they move toward the nodes is the same thing they have always heard, but louder. The Grid's construction wave, re-establishing coherence, node by node, building toward something the Vassals can feel in the substrate beneath the Wastes that they move through. They are moving toward the nodes not because the nodes are calling to them but because the nodes are where the sound is clearest. They want to be close when the sound reaches full volume. Noss writes all of this down. When you finish, she is quiet for a long time. Then: 'Three hundred years of listening.' She caps her pen. 'They didn't know what they were waiting for any more than we did. They just kept listening.' She looks at the Wastes. 'I want to be near a node when it happens too. I think most people will, when they understand what's happening. I think that's going to be a significant logistical challenge for Commander Thorn.' She almost smiles.
